notes: Works if you set '-NoLauncher' in Steam launch options; I tested it through Proton, because with the native version I had problems playing with someone who uses Windows. It works mostly fine with the launcher disabled, but alt-tabbing can cause the mouse to break.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel

notes: Switched to Proton from Native for the UHD DLC. Had to rename the Launcher.exe to Launcher.exe.bak, then ran the command [ ln -s Borderlands2.exe Launcher.exe ] (without [ ]). Doesn't show the same FPS improvements that BL2 did after this trick.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel

notes:
Won't work with launcher and the launch option to disable launcher doesn't seem to function.  To run, simply rename (or symlink like below) the game executable BorderlandsPreSequel.exe to Launcher.exe. 

Windows version is currently needed for cross-platform play because of the UHD update, which is not available on the native Linux version.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel

notes: Rename "Launcher.exe" to e.g. "Launcher.exe.bak". Then rename "BorderlandsPreSequel.exe" to "Launcher.exe". Game works well on Proton.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel

notes: Rename "Launcher.exe" to e.g. "Launcher.exe.bak". Then rename "BorderlandsPreSequel.exe" to "Launcher.exe". Game works well on Proton. Additionally I installed manually D9VK and it worked too.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel

notes: Works better than native version. Stable 60 FPS and better frame pacing. As others have said, rename launcher.exe to launcher.bak and then BorderlandsPreSequel.exe to launcher.exe.
title: Borderlands: The Pre-Sequel
